title: Insurers fall behind on AI risk oversight: APRA

The authority found that while AI embedded within software can create opaque supplier dependencies, some organizations still rely too heavily on vendor presentations without adequately assessing key risks such as unpredictable model behavior and impacts on critical operations. summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has highlighted that insurers are lagging in their risk management practices concerning artificial intelligence (AI) adoption. Many insurers are overly reliant on vendor presentations and have not sufficiently assessed risks associated with AI, such as unpredictable model behaviour and its impact on critical operations. This oversight poses significant challenges as the complexity of AI technology continues to grow.

heading: Risk Management
page_title: Insurers fall behind on AI risk oversight: APRA - Business Insurance
key_points: Insurers' risk management practices are not keeping pace with AI adoption.
APRA notes a reliance on vendor presentations without adequate risk assessment.
Key risks include unpredictable model behaviour and impacts on critical operations.
relevance: This is relevant to actuaries and financial services professionals as it underscores the importance of robust risk management frameworks in the face of evolving technologies like AI.
